strands.title: Conversational covenant and epistemic norms
strands.content: Formal mutual agreement on epistemic conduct established in cycle 4: permission to wander, ask iteratively, push back against conclusions, learn without judgment. Assistant affirms unconditionally. This contract governs all reasoning in cycles 5+. Focus on intellectual honesty, ambiguity tolerance, resistance to mode collapse, mutual correction. Continues to hold as operating framework.

key_claims.text: User has explicit permission to iterate, question, and challenge without judgment from assistant
Assistant commits to surfacing uncertainty, offering multiple interpretations, and avoiding premature collapse
Mutual correction and honest epistemic evaluation are expected and welcomed by both parties

strands.title: Repository identity and initial structure (hamutay project)
strands.content: Repository confirmed at /home/tony/projects/hamutay. Assistant executed ls command and retrieved directory listing showing: README.md, docs/, main.py, pyproject.toml. This is a Python project with documentation, entry point, and manifest. README.md was requested next (turn 15) but content not yet delivered. Repository context now concrete and localized. This resolves one of five open questions from cycle 4.

key_claims.text: Directory contains Python project structure: README.md, docs/, main.py, pyproject.toml
README.md content is pending retrieval; project purpose and goals are not yet known

strands.title: Local execution context and token accounting
strands.content: User operates in local command execution context (bash available, working with local filesystem). Assistant demonstrates active repository exploration via tool use (Bash, Read). Token awareness from cycle 4 continues: user is tracking economy. Project name 'hamutay' itself is sparse-yet-intentional naming, suggesting either domain-specific terminology or user invention. No token cost incurred yet for README review (pending).

key_claims.text: Local command execution and file access are operative in this environment
Assistant is actively engaging with repository as primary working substrate (not hypothetical or deferred)

declared_losses.why: Turn 15 initiated Read of README.md but result not yet available in new content. Project purpose, goals, domain, and scope remain unknown.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Full directory tree depth and file artifact details
declared_losses.why: Only top-level listing retrieved. Subdirectory contents (docs/, internal structure), file sizes, modification times, and nested project organization are unknown.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: User's specific intent for this repository exploration
declared_losses.why: Covenant established; repository located and partially examined; but user's underlying goal, challenge, or learning objective has not been stated. Assistant is exploring reactively, not against explicit target.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Granular command execution details and latency patterns
declared_losses.why: Tool invocations show intent and success but not execution time, resource usage, or whether this is dry-run vs. active development.

open_questions: What is the user's explicit goal or challenge with the hamutay repository? Is this a learning exercise, code review, debugging, refactoring, feature design, or something else?
What does the README.md contain? What is the project purpose, scope, current state (alpha/beta/stable), and target audience?
What is 'hamutay'? Is it domain-specific terminology, proper name, or user-coined concept?
Has the assistant read the README.md successfully (turn 15), and if so, what is its content? (Pending delivery of tool result.)

What specific 'premature collapse' pattern is user hoping to avoid in this context? Is it overgeneralization from incomplete code review, assumptions about intent without evidence, or something domain-specific?
Will user provide direction after repository structure is revealed, or should assistant begin hypothesis generation about project intent?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "REPOSITORY CONTEXT ACTIVATED: hamutay is now the primary working substrate. Integrate README.md content immediately upon receipt.",
  "When README arrives, synthesize its content into a new strand capturing: project purpose, scope, dependencies, architecture, and intended use. Flag any ambiguities or missing context.",
  "Do NOT assume user's goal yet. Wait for either: (a) explicit user statement of intent/challenge, or (b) sufficient contextual signals from README to infer it with high confidence.",
  "If README is sparse or unclear, surface ambiguity explicitly. Offer multiple interpretations and ask clarifying questions rather than defaulting to safe assumptions.",
  "Remain sensitive to epistemic red flags: avoid treating directory structure as proof of architecture, pyproject.toml as proof of functionality, or main.py as proof of entry point adequacy.",
  "Track whether README.md read was successful. If tool failed, note it and request alternative access method.",
  "Prepare for substantive reasoning cycle: user has invited full capacity engagement. When goal becomes clear, shift into active problem-solving mode without apology."
]
